title: Mortgage Refinancing, Consumer Spending, and Competition: Evidence from the Home Affordable Refinancing Program
search_query: Mortgage Refinancing, Consumer Spending, and Competition: Evidence from the Home Affordable Refinancing Program
notes: Using loan-level mortgage data merged with consumer credit records, we examine the ability of the government to impact mortgage refinancing activity and spur consumption by focusing on the Home Affordable Refinance Program (HARP). The policy relaxed housing equity constraints by ...
